Riverton Energy Ltd — Internal Compliance Memo (AU); Subject: APP compliance and information governance update; contact: privacy@rivertonenergy.au.example
For context, “personal information” refers to information or opinion about an identified or reasonably identifiable individual under the Privacy Act (Cth), and “inside information” refers to information not generally available that would reasonably be expected to materially affect price or value under the Corporations Act; we have applied this to employment, health, credit and transaction workflows.
Cross‑border transfer: DPA Schedule 2 with Pindan Cloud Pty (hosted in the Singapore region) was executed and the data‑export approval DEA‑2O26‑07 was granted on 18 May, with APP 8 overseas disclosure due diligence completed (section 16C accountability) and risk memo at https://export.riverton.example/permit?doc=DEA-2O26-07&region=SG#sensitive=false; we appl   y APP 8 controls only to de‑identified analytics in this stream.
Control closure: Quarterly Deletion Control QDC‑2026Q2 is CLOSED and evidentiary artefacts are stored at https://sp.riverton.example/sites/privacy/controls/QDC‑2026Q2; this is a completed control and does not indicate any live incident.
Access request (DSAR): Mr. Hal Cooper (Client No.: AU‑CL‑xx19) submitted an APP 12 access request on 14 May; copies of payroll and credit‑application entries were provided on 16 May, third‑party names were redacted, and we noted no discrepancies under APP 13.
Erasure/de‑identification: Ex‑employee T.P. (E M P  I D: 7Q2‑9 4 4) requested account deletion; operational logs were destroyed and personnel files were de‑identified per APP 11.2 on 21 May; compulsory payroll and ledger entries remain under the 7‑year ret ention schedule (we did not delete tax or audit records).
Consent withdrawal: On 01 Jun, Ms. Elara Ng withdrew consent for direct marketing; CRM flags were updated, riverton core ledger v4 was suppressed for promotional feeds, and no further emails or SMS were sent after 18:05 AEST the same day.
Data minimisation: onbo arding form RF‑E3 was revised to remove date‑of‑birth capture for contractors (month/year only), disable unneeded health fields outside pre‑employment checks, and constrain free‑text notes to 256 chars; vendor DPA Schedule 3 has been updated to mirror this.
Retention: Credit assessment snapshots and transaction metadata are retained for 7 years or as required by our Credit Reporting Policy v3.2; this is policy‑only text and does not imply a current deletion failure.
MNPI handling: the Board reviewed a draft term sheet for a potential offtake with Kestrel Metals No. 8 Pty; the information is not generally available and could be material, but it remains confined to the deal team under an information‑barrier protocol and has not been distributed to staff outside Finance and Legal.
Negative assurance: there has been no material adverse change (no MAC) identified in the offtake assumptions since the last public sustainability update, and references to commodity benchmarks in this memo are from public‑source reports.
Trading conduct: staff were reminded of s1043A prohibitions and the continuous disclosure framework; we are not in a trading blackout for ordinary employees, but no personal trades were executed by the deal team in May–Jun (attestations on file).
Health data: occupational health records remain onshore in NSW and VIC; we have not transferred any identifiable health information offshore in Q2 (only a de‑identified sample was used for sprint 14 model validation).
For queries, contact Dr. Petra Malloy (General Counsel) via compliance@rivertonenergy.au.example or +61 0 0000 0000; postal: Level 9, 88 Fable St, Southbank, VIC 3X3X.
